Biography

A Musician's
Musician

Denny Ilett is a self-taught guitarist, vocalist, bandleader, composer, arranger and one of the most versatile performers working in British jazz today. His musical world spans jazz, blues, big band, funk, soul, rock and psychedelia — often within a single show.

Over four decades, Denny has built a career defined by extraordinary collaborations, an unflinching curiosity, and a commitment to music that genuinely moves people. From Ronnie Scott's to international festival stages, the quality of his work has earned him acclaim from audiences and critics alike.

"A knockout guitarist — soulful and jazz-tinged."
— GUITARIST MAGAZINE

Electric Lady Big Band — Official Showreel
The Flagship Project

Electric Lady
Big Band

Founded in 2018 to celebrate the release of Jimi Hendrix's landmark 1968 album Electric Ladyland, the Electric Lady Big Band is an acclaimed 16-piece ensemble led by Denny Ilett.

The group reimagines Hendrix's iconic catalogue with power, precision and deep respect — drawing on jazz, psychedelia, blues, rock and funk to create something genuinely unlike anything else on the circuit.

Two studio albums. Performances at Ronnie Scott's and major UK jazz festivals. National recognition. And a sound that has to be heard live to be fully believed.

Book the Band Listen on Bandcamp
The Details
FormationFounded 2018, Bristol
Line-up16-piece ensemble — brass, rhythm section, guitar and vocals
VenuesRonnie Scott's, major UK jazz festivals, theatres and arts centres nationwide
RecordingsTwo studio albums of reimagined Hendrix material
RepertoireElectric Ladyland in full, plus tracks from across Hendrix's catalogue — all reimagined as big band arrangements
MusiciansSome of the finest jazz players in the UK
Cheltenham Jazz Festival  ·  Photo: John Watson / London Jazz News
Craft

Guitarist &
Arranger

The Guitarist

Self-taught · Jazz · Blues · Rock · Soul · Funk
A self-taught guitarist with a voice entirely his own, Denny's playing defies easy categorisation. Guitarist Magazine called him "a knockout guitarist — soulful and jazz-tinged," and that precision captures something essential: a player whose technique serves emotion rather than the other way around.

His guitar work spans jazz improvisation, blues expression, big band comping and beyond — always in service of the music, never at its expense.
Pee Wee Ellis Adrian Utley Will Gregory Bobby Shew Paul Jones Lewis Taylor Lillian Boutté Charles Hazlewood

The Arranger

Big Band · Orchestral · Contemporary · Cross-genre
Denny's arranging work is central to both the Electric Lady Big Band and his Sinatra shows. His charts blend intricate brass writing, rich harmonies and driving rhythmic feel — giving familiar songs a new life that feels both respectful and completely fresh.

His orchestral work extends beyond jazz: he has been involved in large-scale contemporary classical and cross-genre projects, conducting as well as arranging, always seeking the unexpected connection between musical worlds.
Big Band Charts Orchestral Contemporary Classical Rock & Funk Film & Stage
Press & Reviews

What They're
Saying

"
A knockout guitarist — soulful and jazz-tinged. Ilett plays with the kind of conviction that reminds you why this music still matters.
Guitarist Magazine
"
The band interprets Hendrix with power, precision, passion and deep respect — a reminder of the true genius of Jimi as composer and musician, a legacy far beyond the popular guitar hero image.
Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club
"
A unique and heartfelt testimony to the music and legacy of Jimi Hendrix. Under the stewardship of Denny Ilett, the Electric Lady Big Band has produced something remarkable. Let it continue to thrive in eternity.
London Jazz News — Frank Griffith
"
It's clear this project is more than just a Hendrix experience. The 16-piece band created a fantastic bursting sound that enveloped the audience — the jazz element was strongly and impressively represented.
UK Jazz News
"
Perhaps this was something like what Hendrix had in mind. An explosive start — the band had a ball from first note to last.
Bebop Spoken Here
"
Ilett's remarkable skills as guitarist, vocalist and arranger successfully realise Hendrix's reputation as a visionary in a large ensemble setting.
Jazz Music Archives
